raw_result.description: NMI-powered payment gateway/connect offering for payment processing and merchant account services.
raw_result.auth_notes: Evidence gathered points to NMI public docs using OAuth 2.0 for newer API docs, while NMI also exposes legacy payment interfaces with key=value style responses; no Paygent Connect-specific auth documentation was verified on a first-party Paygent Connect site, so auth is inferred from NMI platform docs only.
raw_result.gating_reason: No verified self-serve Paygent Connect developer portal or signup flow was found from first-party evidence; the assigned hint indicates an NMI-powered/white-label product, and access appears tied to merchant/partner onboarding rather than open self-serve developer access.

api_surface.breadth: Public NMI docs show a broad payments platform with payment processing, merchant sign-up, account management, and reporting APIs, including v5 payment endpoints; however, Paygent Connect-specific API scope was not directly verified.
api_surface.mcp_notes: A GitHub repository named FoxRick/paygentic-sdk was found and described as an MCP server related to PayGent, but it appears third-party and not official; no official Paygent Connect or NMI MCP was verified.

raw_result.blocker: Toolkit build appears possible against underlying NMI APIs, but Paygent Connect-specific docs, auth mapping, and self-serve access were not directly verified from first-party pages.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official NMI docs include a rate limiting page describing payment-API-specific throttling with response_code 301 and system-wide HTTP 429 limits. No Paygent Connect-specific limits were verified.
raw_result.notable_signals: The provided Paygent URL hint could not be resolved to a verified first-party Paygent Connect site during research. Public evidence instead centered on NMI documentation, suggesting the product may be white-labeled or reseller-managed. An MCP-like repo exists on GitHub, but only as a third-party signal.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Critical fields depend on inference from NMI platform docs rather than a verified first-party Paygent Connect developer/access page. The exact official Paygent Connect domain, auth method, access tier, and API surface for this branded app were not directly confirmed.

result.gating_reason: No verified self-serve Paygent Connect developer portal or signup flow was found from first-party evidence; the assigned hint indicates an NMI-powered/white-label product, and access appears tied to merchant/partner onboarding rather than open self-serve developer access.

api_surface.breadth: Public NMI docs show a broad payments platform with payment processing, merchant sign-up, account management, and reporting APIs, including v5 payment endpoints; however, Paygent Connect-specific API scope was not directly verified.
api_surface.mcp_notes: A GitHub repository named FoxRick/paygentic-sdk was found and described as an MCP server related to PayGent, but it appears third-party and not official; no official Paygent Connect or NMI MCP was verified.

result.blocker: Toolkit build appears possible against underlying NMI APIs, but Paygent Connect-specific docs, auth mapping, and self-serve access were not directly verified from first-party pages.
result.rate_limit_notes: Official NMI docs include a rate limiting page describing payment-API-specific throttling with response_code 301 and system-wide HTTP 429 limits. No Paygent Connect-specific limits were verified.

Public evidence instead centered on NMI documentation, suggesting the product may be white-labeled or reseller-managed. An MCP-like repo exists on GitHub, but only as a third-party signal.
